title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Glass is one of the most versatile materials ever discovered, and its history (0) back thousands of years. The earliest known glass objects were beads made in ancient Mesopotamia around 3500 BCE. For centuries, glass remained a luxury item, as the manufacturing process was complex and required (1) skill. The invention of glassblowing around the first century BCE (2) everything, making it possible to produce vessels quickly and cheaply. The Romans became masters of this craft, creating beautiful objects that were traded throughout their empire. During the Middle Ages, European craftsmen developed techniques for making stained glass windows, which (3) magnificent cathedrals across the continent. The island of Murano in Venice became famous for its exquisite glasswork, with secrets (4) down through generations of artisans. The Industrial Revolution brought mass production, making glass (5) to ordinary people for the first time. Today, glass is everywhere in our lives, from the screens of our smartphones to the windows of our buildings. Modern glass technology has produced materials with remarkable properties, including glass that can (6) itself when cracked and glass that generates electricity from sunlight. Scientists continue to push the (7) of what glass can do, developing new applications in medicine, construction, and communications. This ancient material remains at the (8) of technological innovation.

example.explanation: 'Dates back' is the correct phrasal verb to indicate that something dates back to a historical period.

questions.explanation: 'Considerable skill' is a common collocation to describe a high level of skill.
'Changed everything' is the most natural expression to indicate a total and significant change.
'Adorned' is the most elegant verb to describe how windows embellished cathedrals.
'Passed down' is the correct phrasal verb to describe the transmission of knowledge between generations.
'Accessible to' is the correct collocation to indicate that something is within reach of people.
'Heal itself' is the expression used for materials that can self-heal.
'Push the boundaries' is a fixed expression that means expanding the limits of what is possible.
'At the forefront of' is the correct expression to indicate that something is at the forefront.
